raw_consumer_statement:
Re : Amex Dispute Ref. XXXX XX/XX/XXXX : I filed a billing dispute with American Express regarding a {$5100.00} charge from XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX. The charge was a prepayment for alleged vehicle damage following an accident. Despite repeated requests over approximately three months, XXXX XXXX failed to provide documentation showing the actual repair costs or a final invoice. I initiated the dispute to remove the charge and avoid ongoing interest accrual. 

XX/XX/XXXX : American Express initially resolved the dispute in my favor and issued a credit. However, they later reversed the decision and re-billed the charge, stating that I had an open rental car insurance claim through my American Express XXXX card benefits. This insurance claim had been filed only as a contingency in case XXXX ultimately provided repair documentation, which never occurred. I was not seeking duplicate reimbursement. 

XX/XX/XXXX Present : I reopened the billing dispute on XX/XX/XXXX. Since then, American Express has repeatedly delayed the stated resolution date first from XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX, and most recently to XX/XX/XXXX. Despite multiple calls requesting clarification ( most recently on XX/XX/XXXX ), American Express has provided no substantive explanation for these delays. The original dispute was resolved in approximately one month, and I have been given no clear reason why this refiled dispute remains unresolved.
